title: NASA to Unveil Complete Roman Telescope, Host Media Briefing
summary: Media are invited Tuesday, April 21, to NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, for a look at the agency’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, which recently completed construction and is wrapping up prelaunch testing. This will be one of the last opportunities to view the fully integrated flagship telescope before it ships to […]

title: NASA Releases Artemis II Moon Mission Launch Countdown
summary: Before NASA sends its astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen on their Artemis II mission around the Moon, the launch team at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida and teams across the country will begin counting down about two days before liftoff.  A launch countdown contains […]

title: Station Nation: Aaron Rose, Cold Stowage Mission Manager
summary: As a member of the Crew and Thermal Systems Division, Aaron Rose supports critical cargo resupply missions to the International Space Station. In this role, he works with payload developers to safely transport temperature-controlled science experiments to and from station with portable coolers, freezers, and refrigerators.  For the full flight cycle, Rose and his team members ensure all cold stowage hardware, operations, and personnel are coordinated to […]

title: Turning growth into profits remains a challenge as space demand grows
summary:
While the space economy is expanding across multiple fronts, questions remain about how some of its most promising opportunities could translate growing demand into sustainable profits.
The post Turning growth into profits remains a challenge as space demand grows appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Xona raises $170 million for satellite navigation network
summary:
The company is scaling up production as it looks to build a 258-satellite network to provide positioning, navigation and timing services
The post Xona raises $170 million for satellite navigation network appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: China sends radar mapping and GNSS augmentation sats into orbit with pair of launches
summary:
China conducted a pair of launches in recent days, adding new satellites to the SuperView and CentiSpace low Earth orbit infrastructure constellations.
The post China sends radar mapping and GNSS augmentation sats into orbit with pair of launches appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Arctic Winter Sea Ice Ties Record Low, NASA, NSIDC Scientists Find
summary: For the second consecutive year, winter sea ice in the Arctic reached a level that matches the lowest peak observed since satellite monitoring began in 1979. On March 15, Arctic sea ice extent reached 5.52 million square miles (14.29 million square kilometers), very close to the 2025 peak of 5.53 million square miles (14.31 million […]

title: NASA’s IXPE Gets Fresh Look at Supernova
summary: NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) mission has taken a new observation of a supernova, RCW 86, seen here in an image released on March 24, 2026. This observation helps fill in a fuller picture of what other telescopes have seen. The full image combines IXPE’s data with legacy observations from two other X-ray telescopes: […]

title: Live Coverage: SpaceX to launch batch of Starlink satellites from the West Coast
summary: Liftoff of the Starlink 17-17 mission from Vandenberg Space Force Base, in California is scheduled during a launch window that opens at 4:03 p.m. PDT (7:03 p.m. EDT / 2303 UTC).
